summary: Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement establishing two pilot zones for Hezbollah disarmament and Lebanese army control. Israeli air forces eliminated seven Hezbollah militants near al-Manzala in southern Lebanon. Iran launched at least four one‑way attack drones at ships in the Strait of Hormuz, hitting one cargo vessel.
key_points: Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement creating two pilot zones near the Litani River for Hezbollah disarmament and Lebanese army control (Source: @lelotsenzura/93246).
Israeli air force struck and killed seven Hezbollah militants who had placed weapons near Israeli forces in al‑Manzala, southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/202100).
The Israeli Defense Forces reported over ten killings in Gaza and six strikes in southern Lebanon during the week (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
More than 100 individuals were arrested in the West Bank, according to IDF reports (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
Iran fired at least four one‑way attack drones at ships in the Strait of Hormuz, one hitting the upper deck of a large cargo ship (Source: @shin_persian/7215).
Three explosions were reported in Sirik, Hormozgan Province, Iran (Source: @shin_persian/7218).
The United States announced a $30 million reimbursement to the Lebanese Armed Forces to strengthen its operational capacity (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4406).
Lebanese police used tear‑gas shells to disperse protesters near the airport (Source: @naya_foriraq/80024).

sections.executive_summary: Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement creating two pilot zones for Hezbollah disarmament and Lebanese army control. Israeli air forces eliminated seven Hezbollah militants near al‑Manzala in southern Lebanon. Iran launched at least four one‑way attack drones at ships in the Strait of Hormuz, hitting one cargo vessel.

points: Iran launched at least four one‑way attack drones at vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz; one drone struck the upper deck of a large cargo ship, while three were downed (Source: @shin_persian/7215).
Three separate explosions were reported in Sirik, Hormozgan Province, Iran, with the source of the blasts not yet confirmed (Source: @shin_persian/7218).
Iranian state media reported an explosion heard in Sirik, reinforcing concerns about regional security (Source: @presstv/195797).
Iranian officials reiterated that the nation's national security and right to self‑defence are non‑negotiable (Source: @presstv/195792).

The Israeli Air Force, guided by Unit Aguz, destroyed a Hezbollah weapons cache and killed seven militants in al‑Manzala, southern Lebanon, removing an immediate threat to Israeli forces (Source: @New_security8200/202100).

Prime Minister Netanyahu emphasized that Israel will maintain its security zone in southern Lebanon until Hezbollah is fully disarmed, describing it as a major blow to Iran (Source: @almogboker78/17597).
The IDF reported more than ten lethal operations in Gaza during the week, indicating continued high‑intensity combat (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
Over 100 arrests were made in the West Bank, reflecting intensified security actions in occupied territories (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
Israel’s defense posture includes unrestricted operational freedom throughout the security zone to eliminate any threats (Source: @lelotsenzura/93246).
During the reporting period, Israeli forces carried out more than ten lethal strikes in Gaza, underscoring ongoing hostilities (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
Israeli security services detained over 100 individuals in the West Bank, indicating a broad crackdown on suspected militants (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
The Israeli Defense Forces continue to operate across Gaza and the West Bank, maintaining pressure on militant networks (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).

Israel, Lebanon, and the United States signed a tripartite framework agreement establishing two pilot zones near the Litani River for Hezbollah disarmament and transfer of control to the Lebanese army (Source: @lelotsenzura/93246).
The United States announced a $30 million reimbursement to the Lebanese Armed Forces to enhance its operational capacity and sovereignty (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4406).
Lebanese police deployed tear‑gas shells to disperse protesters at the airport, reflecting internal unrest (Source: @naya_foriraq/80024).
Continued hostile aircraft activity over Lebanon was reported, urging caution among residents (Source: @redlinkleb/28450).
Lebanese officials rejected U.S. and Israeli proposals for “experimental zones,” insisting on Lebanese army presence throughout the area (Source: @yediotnews25/131957).
The United Arab Emirates resolved a technical glitch in its emergency alert system, unrelated to conflict operations (Source: @shin_persian/7214).

key_developments: Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement creating two pilot zones for Hezbollah disarmament and Lebanese army control near the Litani River (Source: @lelotsenzura/93246).
Israeli air force eliminated seven Hezbollah militants and a weapons cache in al‑Manzala, southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/202100).
Iran launched at least four one‑way attack drones at ships in the Strait of Hormuz, one striking a cargo vessel (Source: @shin_persian/7215).
IDF reported over ten lethal operations in Gaza and six strikes in southern Lebanon during the week (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
More than 100 arrests were made in the West Bank, indicating intensified security actions (Source: @idf_telegram/24448).
Three explosions were reported in Sirik, Hormozgan Province, Iran (Source: @shin_persian/7218).
The United States pledged $30 million to the Lebanese Armed Forces to boost its operational capacity (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4406).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah remains a proximate threat to Israel's southern border, prompting Israeli air strikes and the establishment of pilot disarmament zones. Iran's drone attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz demonstrate its willingness to project power beyond its borders, raising maritime security concerns. Ongoing Israeli operations in Gaza and the West Bank sustain a high‑intensity conflict environment, while internal Lebanese unrest and external pressure from the United States add complexity to the Lebanon‑Israel dynamic.
sections.regional_response: The United States is actively facilitating diplomatic frameworks between Israel and Lebanon while providing financial support to the Lebanese Armed Forces, aiming to stabilize the border. Iran continues to assert its regional influence through drone deployments and rhetoric, challenging U.S. and Israeli interests. Gulf states, exemplified by the UAE, are managing domestic security issues but remain peripheral to the core conflict dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah's weapon transfers in southern Lebanon is critical as Israel expands its security zone. Iranian drone activity in the Strait of Hormuz requires enhanced maritime surveillance to protect commercial vessels. The large number of arrests in the West Bank suggests a broadening of Israeli security operations, potentially impacting civilian dynamics and resistance activities.
